preamble: Deaffrication of the voiced alveolar affricate [dʒ] to the voiced alveolar plosive [d] is a sound change that eliminates the fricative release component while preserving voicing and place. This process occurs both as a phonological change and in synchronic variation across multiple language families.

examples.note: Deaffrication in early child language development, commonly occurring in words like 'judge' → [ˈdʌd].
Deaffrication in rapid or casual speech, affecting the realization of borrowed affricates.
Dialectal deaffrication of word-initial [dʒ] in some Brazilian Portuguese varieties.
